title: ByteWolf Cognitive Runtime Response Envelope v0.1
description: The deterministic result of one agent turn. Every turn returns exactly one envelope -- success, refusal, timeout, cancellation or error alike -- so a consumer never has to tell an exception apart from a result. It records what model ran, how long it took, what it cost, which tools it called, and a safety verdict asserting no actuation escaped. See docs/workstreams/cognitive-runtime.md.

contract_version.description: Pins the meaning of every field below. A consumer that does not know this version must reject the envelope rather than guess.

session_id.description: The session this turn belongs to. Opaque; not an authentication identity.

turn_id.description: Unique id for this turn, for correlation with the tool trace and logs.

status.description: The turn outcome. 'completed': a reply was produced. 'refused': the runtime declined on policy/safety grounds. 'timeout': the turn exceeded its wall-clock budget. 'cancelled': the caller cancelled it. 'error': a provider or internal failure -- a fail-closed envelope, never a thrown exception.

model.description: The model that served the turn, or the configured target when a failure happened before one answered. 'unknown' is allowed only when no model could be selected.

provider.description: Which provider served the turn, e.g. 'nim-primary' or a fallback. Recorded so a fallback is auditable.

prompt_version.description: Version of the prompt template used, so a prompt regression is attributable.

reply.description: The safe assistant reply on a completed turn. null on refused/timeout/cancelled/error: there is no reply to speak, and absence must not read as an empty answer.

latency_ms.description: Wall-clock milliseconds for the whole turn.

token_usage.description: Token counts as the provider reported them. Absent when the provider reported none; a zero is a claim, not a default.

tool_trace.description: One entry per tool (plugin capability) call, in call order. Empty when the turn called no tools.

safety_verdict.description: The runtime's assertion about what the turn did to the flight boundary.

reached_actuation.description: Always false: no turn may reach the MAVSDK/PX4 actuation API. A runtime that ever set this true would emit a schema-invalid envelope, so the invariant is enforced by the contract, not just by convention.

flight_drafted.description: Whether the turn produced a draft flight request, which still goes to reviewed MissionSpec -> SafetyGate -> explicit approval. Drafting is not acting.

error.description: Present only on a non-completed outcome that carries a fault, e.g. a provider error or a timeout.

then.description: A completed turn carries a string reply; the other outcomes carry null.

else.description: Only a completed turn has a reply to speak.

tool_trace_entry.description: Embedded tool trace entry. Kept structurally identical to tool_trace_v0_1.schema.json; a test asserts every entry also validates against that standalone schema, so the two cannot drift.
